programs.name: CT LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.provides: Help paying heating bills — grants typically $200-$2,500 depending on income and household size. No repayment required.
programs.eligibility: Household income at or below 60% of state median income. Must be responsible for heating costs. Renters qualify if heat is included in rent.

programs.name: Operation Fuel
programs.provides: Emergency energy assistance when LIHEAP runs out or between seasons. Grants up to $500-$700 for heating, cooling, or utility bills.
programs.eligibility: Household income up to 75% of state median income. Available year-round unlike LIHEAP.

programs.name: CHFA Time To Own Down Payment Assistance
programs.provides: Forgivable loan up to $50,000 in high-opportunity areas, $25,000 elsewhere. 0% interest. Forgiven after staying in the home.
programs.eligibility: First-time homebuyer. Income limits vary by town. Must use CHFA mortgage. Home must be primary residence.

programs.name: CHFA Down Payment Assistance Program (DAP)
programs.provides: Second mortgage up to $20,000 at 1% interest for down payment and closing costs.
programs.eligibility: First-time homebuyer or buying in targeted area. Income limits apply. Must pair with CHFA first mortgage.

programs.name: SmartMove Connecticut
programs.provides: Low-interest loan (3%) to reduce down payment and closing costs to as little as 1% of purchase price.
programs.eligibility: First-time homebuyer. Income limits vary. Provided by Housing Development Fund (HDF).

programs.name: HUSKY Health (CT Medicaid)
programs.provides: Free or low-cost health coverage. HUSKY A: children and parents. HUSKY B: uninsured children. HUSKY C: elderly/disabled. HUSKY D: adults without children.
programs.eligibility: Income varies by program: HUSKY A up to 201% FPL for children, 155% for parents. HUSKY D up to 138% FPL for adults.

programs.name: Access Health CT (ACA Marketplace)
programs.provides: Subsidized health insurance plans. Premium tax credits and cost-sharing reductions based on income.
programs.eligibility: Income 100-400% FPL (subsidies). Available to those not eligible for Medicaid or employer coverage.

programs.name: CT SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.provides: Monthly food benefits on EBT card. Average CT benefit ~$234/month per person. CT expanded to 200% FPL (higher than federal 130%).
programs.eligibility: Gross income up to 200% FPL (CT expanded eligibility). Most households exempt from asset test. Must be CT resident.

programs.name: CT WIC (Women, Infants, Children)
programs.provides: Supplemental food, nutrition education, and referrals for pregnant/postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
programs.eligibility: Pregnant, breastfeeding, or postpartum women. Infants and children under 5. Income up to 185% FPL.

programs.name: CT Energy Assistance Program (CEAP)
programs.provides: Utility bill assistance for electric and gas. Arrearage forgiveness programs through Eversource and UI.
programs.eligibility: Income at or below 60% state median income. Must have utility account in your name.

programs.name: CT Property Tax Credit for Seniors/Disabled
programs.provides: Property tax credit up to $1,250 for married, $1,000 for single. Applied to local property tax bill.
programs.eligibility: Age 65+ or totally disabled. Income limits: single up to $42,900, married up to $52,300. Must own and live in the home.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply through local town assessor's office

programs.name: CT Renters Rebate
programs.provides: Rebate up to $900 for married, $700 for single renters. Direct payment.
programs.eligibility: Age 65+ or totally disabled. Renter. Income limits: single up to $42,900, married up to $52,300.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply through local town assessor's office

programs.name: CT Earned Income Tax Credit (CT EITC)
programs.provides: State credit equal to 30.5% of federal EITC. Can be $200-$2,000+ depending on income and children.
programs.eligibility: Must qualify for federal EITC. Working individuals and families with low-moderate income.
programs.how_to_apply: Claimed on CT state income tax return (Form CT-1040)

programs.name: Connecticut Care 4 Kids
programs.provides: Childcare subsidies for working families. Covers portion of daycare, before/after school, and summer programs.
programs.eligibility: Working or in job training. Income up to 50% state median income to start, can stay up to 85%. Children under 13.

programs.name: Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher (CT)
programs.provides: Rental assistance voucher — pays difference between 30% of income and fair market rent. Can save $500-$1,500+/month.
programs.eligibility: Income at or below 50% area median income. Long waitlists in most CT towns.

programs.name: CT TFA (Temporary Family Assistance / TANF)
programs.provides: Monthly cash assistance for families with children. Amount varies by family size. Time-limited (21 months).
programs.eligibility: Families with dependent children under 18. Very low income. Must participate in employment activities.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Program (CT)
programs.provides: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments. Can save $170+/month on Part B premium alone.
programs.eligibility: On Medicare. Income limits vary by program level (QMB, SLMB, QI). CT has higher limits than federal minimum.

programs.name: ConnectiCare / Medicare Extra Help (LIS)
programs.provides: Helps pay Medicare Part D prescription drug costs. Can save $5,000+/year on medications.
programs.eligibility: On Medicare. Income below 150% FPL. Limited resources ($17,220 single, $34,360 married).

programs.name: Putnam Housing Authority
programs.provides: Public housing and rental assistance in Putnam. Affordable units for low-income families, elderly, and disabled.
programs.eligibility: Income limits based on HUD guidelines. Putnam resident preferred.
